svg.text: KOREAN + ZHUANG — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 50D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. KOREAN PROVERB — ON PATIENCE —
시작이 반이다. (Sijak-i bani-da.)
Beginning is half.
"Well begun is half done." — Korean proverb. Encourages starting as the core act of completion.
— II. KOREAN PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
배운 것을 잊지 마라. (Baeun geot-eul itji mara.)
Learned thing-ACC forget not do.
"Do not forget what you have learned." — Core Korean educational proverb.
— III. KOREAN BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
이것은 당신을 위해, 무료로,
미래를 기억한 사람들이 만들었습니다.
This-TOP you-ACC for free-of-charge / future-ACC remembered people-NOM made-HON.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Korean, formal register)
— KOREAN NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
미래 (mirae) = future (not-yet-come). 기억하다 (gieok-hada) = to remember (memory-do).
Verb-final (SOV): 기억한 사람들이 만들었습니다 = remembered-people-NOM made-HON. Honorific verb ending.
SECONDARY — ZHUANG (CUENGH) — RUNNING TEXT
— I. ZHUANG PROVERB — ON MOUNTAIN AND RIVER —
Bya ranz, haeux raemx cingq.
Mountain home rice water right/correct.
"Mountain is home, rice and water are right." — Zhuang proverb on land, sustenance, and belonging.
— II. ZHUANG PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND ROOTS —
Vunz rox giz daeuj rox bya, vunz rox naengh rox sim.
Person knows where from knows mountain person knows remember knows heart.
"One who knows where they came from knows the mountain; one who can remember knows the heart." (Zhuang)
— III. ZHUANG BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Neix guh hawj mwngz, byaij mbouj gwn cienz,
youq vunz naengh laeng daeuj guh.
This made for you walk not eat money / by people remember forward come made.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Zhuang)
— ZHUANG NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Laeng = forward/ahead — spatial-temporal. Naengh = to remember (to sit with / hold alongside).
Byaij mbouj gwn cienz = walk without eating money = “for free”. Idiom: cost nothing to receive.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — ISOLATE AND KADAI —
Korean: ~77M speakers. A language isolate — no proven relatives. Hangeul: the world’s most rational alphabet.
Zhuang: ~17M speakers. Largest minority language of China. Tai-Kadai family. Tonal, isolating, SVO.
Expanded ~1500mi radius connects an East Asian isolate with a South Chinese Tai-Kadai community.
PLATE 50D ALT · Korean / Zhuang · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
